repo_prior_art.cite: parent only has one-cutoff retention program; no other catalogue many-neighborhood shared retained-mass object found

published_prior_art.cite: gerard_rokkanen_rothe_2020_rdbounds and rosenman_rajkumar_gauriot_slonim_2019_discrete_manipulation cover manipulated-RD bounds but not a shared nested-window retained-mass image
bertanha_2020_many_thresholds and cattaneo_titiunik_vazquez_bare_2020_rdmulti handle many-threshold/multicutoff RD without manipulation-driven partial-ID flow certificates
chernozhukov_lee_rosen_2013_intersection_bounds supplies generic intersection-bound inference, not an RD-specific dual oracle or joint-versus-separate manipulation frontier

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: Computation-axis delta is only partial: the draft names a lifted LP and TU/min-cost-flow certificate, but the certificate is not coherently exhibited, so the delta_summary overclaims a delivered strongly-polynomial class.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship many-cutoff software comparison cites cattaneo_titiunik_vazquez_bare_2020_rdmulti and cattaneo_jansson_ma_2020_density without a section, command, page, or equation anchor tying them to the claimed computation gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The §4 result entry's Consumer clause is generic: 'pooled manipulation-robust RD sensitivity analysis' names no downstream paper, software workflow, applied domain, or open question.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The §4 result entry's Consumer clause is generic: 'inference for joint manipulation-robust RD frontiers' does not name a downstream consumer.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed full TU matrix includes covariate-share rows with arbitrary rational pi coefficients and score rows with y_b-lambda_j d_b coefficients; consecutive support alone does not make such a matrix totally unimodular.

soundness_flags.label: Exhibit 9.1
soundness_flags.one_line: With n=(1,0), lambda=(1,1/2), and zero left means, the outer score is 1/2, so the claimed zero slack in both score inequalities is false.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the TU claim with an exact network-flow formulation whose constraint matrix is genuinely incidence-based, or explicitly state the extra restrictions on pi, outcomes, and lambda needed for TU.
Repair Exhibit 9.1 by computing the full LP_lambda optimum, including score slack, on a nontrivial K>1 instance where the covariate-balance rows are present.
Add location-level anchors for rdmulti commands and lpdensity/manipulation diagnostics, then name those workflows directly as consumers in the §4 result entries.

recommended_next_step: Revise the computation certificate and worked exhibit before sending this to Stage 0; the novelty survives, but the flagship upgrade is not yet internally coherent.

upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The proposal adds an explicit lifted program and boundary case relative to the parent, but the TU/min-cost-flow certificate and exhibit do not yet validate the claimed computation upgrade.
